name: Topic Selection and Scoring Engine Plan
purpose: Define the non-active topic selection and scoring engine for Drishvara episodic knowledge production, consuming AG24A doctrine, AG23G scoring logic, AG23F source verification rules, and AG23Z homepage/First Light closure.

consumed_source_of_truth.label: First Light Source and Verification Plan
Homepage Daily Surface and First Light Closure

source_doctrine_consumption.AG24A: Uses the episodic doctrine to keep Tuesday Learning, Friday World Lens/Burning Topic, and Sunday Deep Read as the primary rhythm.
source_doctrine_consumption.AG23G: Extends the First Light topic scoring model into episodic candidate scoring without replacing the completed AG23G model.
source_doctrine_consumption.AG23F: Applies manual/curated source verification and blocks unsupported breaking-news claims.
source_doctrine_consumption.AG23Z: Preserves homepage daily surface closure boundaries and keeps AG24B non-mutating and non-publishing.

topic_intake_types.selection_note: Promote only when the subject can be broken into teachable episodes and remains useful beyond current news cycles.
Promote only when credible sources are available and the topic can be handled with context, continuity and caution.
Promote when the topic improves public understanding of systems, institutions and delivery mechanisms.
Promote when the topic supports Drishvara's vision-reflection-insight identity without unsupported claims.
Promote only when the First Light signal score and source status justify conversion into a brief or episode candidate.

criteria.label: Current relevance
criteria.scoring_question: Is the topic active, important or timely now?
criteria.interpretation: Higher score means the topic is currently visible, consequential or being actively discussed.

criteria.label: Evergreen value
criteria.scoring_question: Will the topic remain useful beyond the current week?
criteria.interpretation: Higher score means the topic can remain valuable even after the immediate event cycle ends.

criteria.label: Audience benefit
criteria.scoring_question: Will the topic teach, clarify or help the reader think better?
criteria.interpretation: Higher score means the topic offers clear educational, reflective or decision-support value.

criteria.label: Reference availability
criteria.scoring_question: Are credible, reachable and relevant sources available?
criteria.interpretation: Higher score requires credible sources; unresolved sources keep the topic under editorial verification.

criteria.label: Episode depth potential
criteria.scoring_question: Can the topic sustain at least 8 to 12 meaningful episodes?
criteria.interpretation: Higher score means the topic has enough conceptual depth, subtopics and continuity for a series.

criteria.label: Visual/object potential
criteria.scoring_question: Can diagrams, tables, infographics, timelines or examples improve the topic?
criteria.interpretation: Higher score means the topic can support object-rich reading without damaging article layout.

criteria.label: Drishvara brand fit
criteria.scoring_question: Does the topic fit Vision, Reflection and Insight?
criteria.interpretation: Higher score means the topic strengthens Drishvara's identity rather than becoming generic content.

criteria.label: Sensitivity risk
criteria.scoring_question: Does the topic involve high factual, political, social, legal, health or spiritual sensitivity?
criteria.interpretation: More negative score means greater risk and stronger editorial caution is required.

criteria.label: Repetition risk
criteria.scoring_question: Is the topic overused, repetitive or too similar to recent content?
criteria.interpretation: More negative score means the topic should be delayed, reframed or merged with another series.

decision_thresholds.action: Promote to AG24C 12-week episode calendar planning, subject to source gate and editorial review.
Keep in governed topic bank for later use, improvement, source completion or better timing.
Do not generate article or episode now; archive rationale and reconsider only if conditions change.

blocked_conditions: Only broken, parked, spam-like or unreachable links are available.
Topic depends on unsupported breaking-news claims.
Topic requires real-time public claims that cannot be verified manually.
Topic risks false scriptural, cultural, medical, legal or political attribution.
reference_quality_requirements: Relevant to the topic and not merely keyword-matched.
Reachable and responsive at the time of review.
Not spam, parked, misleading or unrelated.
Prefer primary, official, academic, institutional or reputable editorial sources where possible.
Record unresolved links as under editorial verification instead of pretending verification is complete.

first_light_bridge.purpose: Use First Light signals as curated inputs into episodic topic selection, not as a random news feed.
first_light_bridge.signal_score_24_or_more: Eligible for article or episode brief review if reference and risk gates pass.
first_light_bridge.signal_score_18_to_23: Keep as First Light-only or topic bank item unless additional depth or references emerge.
first_light_bridge.signal_score_below_18: Archive or ignore unless the editor manually overrides with documented rationale.

candidate_record_schema_plan.topic_id: Stable slug or deterministic id for candidate tracking.
candidate_record_schema_plan.title: Working title of candidate topic.
candidate_record_schema_plan.topic_type: One of the approved topic intake types.
candidate_record_schema_plan.intended_lane: Tuesday Learning, Friday World Lens, Sunday Deep Read or Featured Read.
candidate_record_schema_plan.source_origin: Manual editor, First Light signal, featured read gap, public systems insight or evergreen bank.
candidate_record_schema_plan.short_context: Brief explanation of why the topic exists.
candidate_record_schema_plan.scores: Object containing all AG24B scoring criteria.
candidate_record_schema_plan.series_depth_note: Evidence that the topic can sustain 8 to 12 episodes if promoted.
candidate_record_schema_plan.visual_object_note: Likely diagrams, tables, infographics, timelines or examples.
candidate_record_schema_plan.risk_flags: Sensitivity, repetition, unsupported claim or source concerns.
candidate_record_schema_plan.homepage_bridge: Whether it can surface in Discover, Read or Reflect movement.
candidate_record_schema_plan.next_stage_candidate: AG24C only when selected as a strong candidate.

workflow_plan.name: candidate_intake
workflow_plan.output: Candidate topic record created from manual input, First Light signal or content gap.

workflow_plan.name: source_gate
workflow_plan.output: Reference availability and verification status checked before scoring is trusted.

workflow_plan.name: risk_screen
workflow_plan.output: Sensitivity and repetition risks recorded before promotion.

workflow_plan.name: score_candidate
workflow_plan.output: All AG24B criteria scored with reason notes.

workflow_plan.name: threshold_decision
workflow_plan.output: Candidate becomes strong series candidate, topic bank item or do-not-use-now item.

workflow_plan.name: series_projection
workflow_plan.output: For strong candidates, outline possible 8 to 12 episode depth.

workflow_plan.name: handoff_to_ag24c
workflow_plan.output: Only strong candidates move to 12-week episode calendar planning.

review_controls.cost_optimization_note: AG24B should score and bank topics before expensive article/image generation or API-backed workflows are used.

next_stage_boundary.allowed_handoff: Only scored topic candidates and planning rules may be handed off. No article generation, publication, backend activation or public mutation is allowed from AG24B.
